sutta: 2 once the exalted 1 was staying near vesali in great grove at the house with a peak roof together with a number of senior monks who were his disciples namely the verbal challa upachala kakata kalimba nikata and kathi saha and other notable monks now on that occasion a crowd of notable lichavis riding in their splendid cars in rivalry with din and upro were dashing into great wood to see the exalted 1 then those vulnerable ones thought here is a crowd of notable lycheevis dashing into great wood to see the exalted 1 now it has been said by the exalted 1 that noise is a thorn to jana suppose we retreat to gosinga wood where is the salah grow there we can stay pleasantly free from noise and crowds stop here for a moment jana here is the perfect the state jana means a state of mental brightness and it is attained when 1's mind becomes 1 pointed and that is perfect concentration in the buddha's teaching so these monks they they said that all this noise is a disturbance to attaining jana is an obstruction of torn to attaining jana so they wanted to go to the salah grove where it was very quiet and they could practice so those vulnerable ones retreated to so to go singapore where is the salah grove and there they stayed pleasantly free from noise and crowds later on the exalted 1 called to the monk saying monks where is chala where is upachala where are kakata and kalimba nikata and kathi saha where are those monks my senior disciples gone and they said as to that sir it occurred to those vulnerable ones here is a crowd of notable lichavis riding in the splendid cars in rivalry within and up raw dashing into great wood to see the exalted 1 now it has been said by the exalted 1 that noise is a thorn to jana suppose we retreat to gosinga wood whereas the salah grove there we can stay pleasantly free from noise and crowds so sir those rainbow ones are gone there there they are staying pleasantly free from noise and crowds and the buddha said well said monks well said those who should assert what those great disciples have asserted would rightly do so indeed monks i have said that noise is a torn to jhana there are these 10 thorns monks what 10 to 1 who delights in seclusion delight in society is a thorn to 1 devoted to concentration on the sign of the unattractiveness concentration on the sign of the attractiveness is a thorn to 1 guarding the doors of the sense faculties the sight of shows is a thorn to the holy life consorting with women is a thorn to the first jhana noise is a torn to the second jhana thought directed and sustained is a torn to the third jhana delight or pity is a torn to the fourth jhana breathing in breathing and out breathing is a thorn through the attainment of cessation of perception and feeling perception and feeling are a thorn lust hatred and delusion are thorns so monks do do you abide thornless do you abide thorn removers do you abide thoughtless thorn removers monks the thornless are arahants the thornless thorn removers are arahants and that's the end of the gupta
commentary:
so here the buddha is saying and for a monk who delights in seclusion delight in society is a torn to 1 devoted to concentration on the sign of the unattractiveness here it means the unattractiveness of the body and then the sign of the attractiveness of the body is a torn to progress in that meditation to 1 guarding the doors of the sense faculties the sight of shows seeing shows like tv and all that is a torn to the holy life consorting with women is a torn and this 1 is is quite a fact because many monks who disrobe they do so because of some women many times among these ropes because of having become too familiar with some women and then some emotion or feeling develops and then sometimes the monk he finds the holy life quite difficult so sometimes they disrobe and marry some woman to the first jhana thoughts noise is a torn if if you want to attain the first jhana you must stay in a quiet place you stay in a city area with a lot of sounds and all that is very difficult to attain to the second jhana thought directed and sustained because thought directed and sustained is is 1 of the factors of the first jhana so when you want to attain to the second jana you must let go of that those thoughts to the third jana the light pity is a torn because in the third jana there's no more delight in the for the fourth jana breathing is a torn because a person who attains a fort jana the breathing stops her and then to the attainment of the cessation of perception and feeling uh perception and feeling are a thorn this cessation of perception and feeling is uh just like nibana where the consciousness stops so uh when the normal sex consciousness when the normal sex consciousness stops her and then this perception and feeling also stopped so if you still have perception and feeling that means you cannot enter that that state of cessation and then lust hatred and delusion so if we till the buddha said if a man gets rid of all these thorns then he can become an arahant
